metadata.description: Challenges validating lazy initialization correctness, caching, thread-safety, and performance benefits across parsers and documents

challenges.name: Parser Lazy Loading Correctness
challenges.description: Verify that registerAllParsersLazy() defers parser instantiation until first access. After lazy registration, getInstantiatedParserCount() must be 0 and getPendingParserCount() must be 17. After accessing each parser, instantiated count must increment by 1 and pending must decrement by 1.

outputs.name: pending_before_access
outputs.description: Pending parser count before any access

outputs.name: instantiated_before_access
outputs.description: Instantiated parser count before any access

outputs.name: pending_after_first_access
outputs.description: Pending count after accessing one parser

outputs.name: instantiated_after_first_access
outputs.description: Instantiated count after accessing one parser

outputs.name: pending_after_all_access
outputs.description: Pending count after accessing all parsers

outputs.name: instantiated_after_all_access
outputs.description: Instantiated count after accessing all parsers

assertions.message: No parsers should be instantiated before first access
All 17 parsers should be pending before access
Exactly 1 parser should be instantiated after first access
No parsers should be pending after all have been accessed
All 17 parsers should be instantiated after full access

challenges.name: Lazy HTML Generation Caching
challenges.description: Verify that ParsedDocument.toHtml() generates HTML lazily on first call and returns cached HTML on subsequent calls. The first call must populate the cache (hasHtmlCached returns true), and clearHtmlCache() must invalidate the cache (hasHtmlCached returns false).

outputs.name: cache_empty_before_first_call
outputs.description: Whether hasHtmlCached was false before first toHtml call

outputs.name: cache_populated_after_first_call
outputs.description: Whether hasHtmlCached was true after first toHtml call

outputs.name: first_call_html
outputs.description: HTML from first toHtml call

outputs.name: second_call_html
outputs.description: HTML from second toHtml call

outputs.name: html_identical
outputs.description: Whether first and second call returned identical HTML

outputs.name: cache_cleared
outputs.description: Whether clearHtmlCache successfully invalidated the cache

assertions.message: Cache must be empty before first toHtml call
Cache must be populated after first toHtml call
Cached HTML must be identical to first generation

challenges.name: Thread-Safe Lazy Initialization
challenges.description: Verify that lazy parser initialization via ParserRegistry is thread-safe. Launch 100 coroutines that simultaneously call getParser() for the same lazily-registered format. Only one parser instance should be created, and all coroutines must receive the same instance.

outputs.name: unique_instances
outputs.description: Number of unique parser instances created

outputs.name: all_same_instance
outputs.description: Whether all coroutines received the same parser instance

outputs.name: coroutines_completed
outputs.description: Number of coroutines that completed successfully

outputs.name: initialization_errors
outputs.description: Any errors during concurrent initialization

assertions.message: Exactly one parser instance must be created (no double initialization)
All coroutines must receive the same parser instance

challenges.name: Lazy Loading Performance Benefit
challenges.description: Measure the startup performance benefit of lazy vs eager parser registration. Compare registerAllParsers() time against registerAllParsersLazy() time. Lazy registration should be at least 50% faster since it defers instantiation.

outputs.name: eager_registration_time_ms
outputs.description: Time for registerAllParsers (eager)

outputs.name: lazy_registration_time_ms
outputs.description: Time for registerAllParsersLazy (lazy)

outputs.name: speedup_factor
outputs.description: Ratio of eager time to lazy time

outputs.name: lazy_faster
outputs.description: Whether lazy registration was faster than eager

assertions.message: Lazy registration must be faster than eager registration
Lazy registration should be at least 1.5x faster than eager
Eager registration time must be measured
Lazy registration time must be measured
